target.title: [codex] Cron: repair empty sanitized final replies
target.body:
﻿???## Summary

- Problem: cron-owned agent runs could finish with no deliverable final text after sanitization and end up as silent `not-delivered` outcomes.
- Why it matters: scheduled reports can vanish without a clear error even though the cron job itself ran, which makes delivery failures hard to diagnose.
- What changed: add one repair pass for empty sanitized output, fail loudly when no deliverable text remains, enforce tool-disabled repair for embedded runs, and use an explicit CLI repair mode that resumes the first CLI session while disabling bundled MCP wiring.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not relax reasoning-tag sanitization, does not change normal non-cron agent turns, and does not address the legacy local WhatsApp health-check cron issue tracked separately in #60204.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Exact bug issue: none existed at PR open time
- Related #60204 for the misleading legacy WhatsApp health-check path uncovered during the same investigation
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: cron delivery could receive an empty final text after sanitization (for example when malformed reasoning tags consumed the visible reply), but the cron runner had no recovery pass and the delivery path did not convert that condition into a hard error.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the runner did not retry with a constrained no-tools finalization prompt, and the direct-delivery path treated missing synthesized text too softly.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): the existing sanitization behavior is intentional; the gap was in cron finalization and delivery handling, not in the sanitizer itself.
- Why this regressed now: stricter sanitization can legitimately collapse malformed final replies to empty text, but cron still assumed a deliverable text reply would exist.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: ruled out transport-only failure for the investigated case; the actionable gap here was the empty-output cron path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts`, `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.owner-auth.test.ts`, `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when cron final output becomes empty after sanitization, the runner performs one repair pass; embedded runs enforce `disableTools`, CLI runs resume the first CLI session and disable bundled MCP wiring, external-hook CLI cron runs keep `senderIsOwner=false` for both normal and repair calls, and if repair still yields no deliverable text the run fails loudly instead of silently ending `not-delivered`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives in cron run finalization plus delivery dispatch, and the narrowest stable guardrail is to exercise those exact seams with mocked agent output.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing interim-retry coverage already covered acknowledgement retries but not empty sanitized output.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Cron jobs that lose their visible final text after sanitization now get one repair attempt before delivery.
- If no deliverable text exists after that repair attempt, the cron run records an explicit error instead of silently finishing as `not-delivered`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[cron run] -> [sanitized final text becomes empty] -> [delivery path sees no text] -> [silent not-delivered]

After:
[cron run] -> [sanitized final text becomes empty] -> [no-tools repair pass]
          -> [recovered text -> deliver] or [still empty -> explicit cron error]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the embedded repair path enforces `disableTools`; the CLI repair path is explicitly not represented as native-tool-free, resumes the first CLI session, and disables OpenClaw-bundled MCP wiring. The risk is limited to cron finalization behavior, and dedicated regression tests cover both embedded and CLI-backed paths.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu Linux
- Runtime/container: local Node + pnpm checkout
- Model/provider: mocked embedded + CLI runner paths in targeted tests
- Integration/channel (if any): cron + Telegram announce path in regression coverage
- Relevant config (redacted): default test fixtures

### Steps

1. Run `pnpm test -- src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts`
2. Run `pnpm test -- src/cron/isolated-agent.skips-delivery-without-whatsapp-recipient-besteffortdeliver-true.test.ts`
3. Run `pnpm test -- src/agents/cli-runner.prepare.test.ts`

### Expected

- Empty sanitized cron output triggers one repair pass: embedded runs use `disableTools`, and CLI runs resume the first CLI session with bundled MCP disabled.
- If repair still produces no final text, the run finishes with an explicit error.
- CLI-backed repair mode does not inject bundled MCP overlays.

### Actual

- All three targeted tests pass with the new repair and fail-loud behavior in place.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: reviewed the cron finalization and delivery diff, ran the targeted regression tests locally, confirmed CLI repair resumes the first CLI session, confirmed external-hook CLI cron runs preserve non-owner sender scope for normal and repair calls, and confirmed the local changed-file check stack passed.
- Edge cases checked: empty payloads without descendants, repair success, repair failure, CLI repair-mode bundled MCP disablement, and first CLI session reuse during repair.
- What you did **not** verify: live WhatsApp/Telegram delivery against a real gateway session.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Cron-owned CLI agent runs that produce no deliverable final text now perform one repair pass and record the repaired final text instead of silently ending without a user-visible result. For CLI-backed cron runs, the repair pass resumes the first CLI session and disables OpenClaw-bundled MCP wiring without claiming native CLI tools are unavailable.

Real environment tested: Windows/PowerShell checkout. The live repair proof below was run at repair head `0f1b25fbfc`; current pushed head `d6ed463c96` keeps that repair path, additionally restores external-hook CLI non-owner sender scope, includes the upstream lint repair needed for the final CI head, and addresses the Greptile duplicate-descendant-predicate review note; verified with targeted red/green tests plus `pnpm lint --threads=8`. Local OpenClaw Gateway was at `ws://127.0.0.1:19162` with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`; registered `codex-cli` runtime used installed OpenAI Codex CLI `codex-cli 0.130.0-alpha.5` through a thin proof wrapper around `codex exec --output-last-message`; cron delivery was disabled intentionally with `--no-deliver`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
corepack pnpm openclaw gateway health --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json

corepack pnpm openclaw cron add --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --name "PR60212 fixed repair marker proof" --at "2030-01-08T00:00:00Z" --keep-after-run --session isolated --message "Real behavior proof for PR 60212. If the prompt you are reading contains the phrase Repair it now, reply exactly OPENCLAW_CRON_REPAIR_OK. Otherwise return an empty final answer and do not output any visible text." --no-deliver --timeout-seconds 300 --model openai/gpt-5.5 --json

corepack pnpm openclaw cron run 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --expect-final --timeout 360000

corepack pnpm openclaw cron show 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json

corepack pnpm openclaw cron runs --id 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --limit 5 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted>
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ corepack pnpm openclaw gateway health --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "eventLoop": { "degraded": false, "reasons": [] },
  "plugins": { "loaded": ["acpx", "browser", "canvas", "device-pair", "file-transfer", "memory-core", "openai", "phone-control", "talk-voice"], "errors": [] }
}

$ corepack pnpm openclaw cron run 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --expect-final --timeout 360000
{
  "ok": true,

$ corepack pnpm openclaw cron show 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted> --json
{
  "id": "3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091",
  "name": "PR60212 fixed repair marker proof",
  "delivery": { "mode": "none", "channel": "last" },
  "state": {
    "lastRunStatus": "ok",
    "lastStatus": "ok",
    "lastDurationMs": 14308,

$ corepack pnpm openclaw cron runs --id 3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091 --limit 5 --url ws://127.0.0.1:19162 --token <redacted>
{
  "entries": [
    {
      "jobId": "3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091",
      "action": "finished",
      "status": "ok",
      "summary": "OPENCLAW_CRON_REPAIR_OK",
      "runId": "manual:3607144d-8660-476d-9e04-6a2825c7f091:1778695539774:2",
```

Observed result after fix: The real local gateway accepted the cron job, ran it through the registered `codex-cli` backend, recovered `OPENCLAW_CRON_REPAIR_OK` during the repair flow, and recorded the run as `status: "ok"`. Delivery was not requested by design because the proof command used `--no-deliver`; the current code keeps the CLI mode explicit, resumes the first CLI session for repair, and does not hit the earlier native-tool fail-closed error.

What was not tested: External WhatsApp/Telegram send was not exercised; delivery was disabled to keep the local proof isolated and non-invasive.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

target.title: fix(agents): accept multi-line ANNOUNCE_SKIP on final line
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix `isAnnounceSkip()` to also accept multi-line sub-agent output where `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` appears as the final line after a newline, not just as the sole content.
- Add focused unit tests for `sessions-send-tokens.ts` covering exact, whitespace-padded, multi-line, and negative cases.
- Add an e2e regression test for the multi-line announce-skip scenario.

Closes #74071

## Problem

Sub-agents that produce a DM summary block followed by `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` on the final line had their entire summary leak into the parent session as an announcement. The strict equality check `(text ?? "").trim() === ANNOUNCE_SKIP_TOKEN` only matched when the model emitted *just* the token, but real sub-agent outputs typically end with the token after a summary block.

## Fix

`isAnnounceSkip()` now checks two conditions:
1. **Exact match** (existing): trimmed text equals the token
2. **Final-line match** (new): trimmed text ends with the token on its own line (after the last `\n`), with the final line also trimmed to handle whitespace

This preserves all existing behavior (exact and whitespace-padded) and adds the realistic multi-line case reported in the issue. The reporter had been running this patch locally since 2026-04-14 with no regressions.

## Test plan

- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tokens.test.ts` — 16 new focused unit tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts` — 9 existing tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts` — 3 existing tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` — 15 existing tests (all pass)

## Notes

- `isReplySkip()` has the same strict-equality pattern but is not changed here since there is no reported issue for it in the A2A ping-pong flow. It could receive the same treatment in a follow-up if needed.
- Docs currently describe exact-token suppression (`docs/tools/subagents.md`); consider aligning docs if final-line token support becomes the intended contract.

target.title: Improve compaction with microCompact pre-pass and analysis scratchpad
target.body:
## Summary

Adopts three techniques to improve compaction quality and reduce LLM cost, addressing #58398:

- **microCompact pre-pass** — Before LLM summarization runs, clears old tool result content from messages whose `toolName` is in the bulky-result set (`read`, `write`, `edit`, `exec`, `bash`, `shell`, `web_search`, `web_fetch`, `browser`). Replaces their content with `[Tool output cleared - not recent]`. The last 5 tool results are preserved (configurable). Error results are never cleared. This is zero LLM cost — pure string replacement on the messages array.

- **Analysis scratchpad in compaction prompt** — Appends instructions asking the model to produce a two-phase response: an `<analysis>` block for chronological reasoning, then a `<summary>` block with the actual summary. The `<analysis>` block is stripped before the summary enters context. This produces better-structured summaries because the model reasons through the conversation before committing to the final output.

- **Post-compact transcript reference** — After compaction, the summary includes a reference to the full session JSONL transcript path so the model can self-serve if it needs details lost in summarization.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for `microCompactMessages` (13 tests) — covers clearable tool names, preserve count, error immunity, metadata preservation, custom preserve count, edge cases
- [x] Unit tests for `stripAnalysisBlock` (8 tests) — covers both-phases present, analysis-only stripping, no-tags fallback, multiline, whitespace trimming
- [x] Unit test for `ANALYSIS_SCRATCHPAD_INSTRUCTIONS` content assertions
- [x] All 146 existing compaction tests pass with zero regressions
- [x] Pre-existing TS errors in `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.ts` confirmed on baseline (not introduced)

target.title: fix(opencode-go): strip per-message reasoning fields for kimi-k2.{5,6} (#83812)
target.body:
Fixes #83812.

`createOpencodeGoKimiNoReasoningWrapper` (`extensions/opencode-go/stream.ts`) already strips root-level `reasoning` / `reasoning_effort` / `reasoningEffort` from outbound requests for opencode-go's Kimi-family models. But stock OpenClaw preserves `messages[].reasoning_details` on replayed assistant turns for these model IDs — `kimi-k2.5` and `kimi-k2.6` are in `REASONING_CONTENT_REPLAY_MODEL_IDS` (`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`), so `shouldPreserveReasoningContentReplay` returns `true` and the assistant-message sanitizer leaves the field intact. After a few turns the provider rejects the request:

```
400 Error from provider: Extra inputs are not permitted,
field: 'messages[5].reasoning_details'
```

The DeepSeek-V4 sibling wrapper next to this one only operates on root-level params, so per-message replay needs its own pass.

## Changes

- `extensions/opencode-go/stream.ts`: extend the existing `stripReasoningParams` payload patch (already gated on `isOpencodeGoKimiNoReasoningModelId`) to walk `payload.messages[]` and `payload.input[]` and strip per-message reasoning fields (`reasoning_details`, `reasoning_content`, `reasoning`, `reasoning_text`). Array-shaped `content` parts on each message are walked recursively. Root-level strip is preserved.
- `extensions/opencode-go/stream.test.ts`: new vitest file with 4 regression cases — replayed `reasoning_details` on `kimi-k2.6`, array-shaped content parts on `kimi-k2.5`, Responses-style `input[]` entries, and a "non-Kimi opencode-go model is untouched" guard.

Diff stat: 2 files, +159 LOC (most of it test coverage; the production change is +44 LOC).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Sanitized issue evidence — opencode-go/kimi-k2.6 returns `400 Extra inputs are not permitted, field: 'messages[N].reasoning_details'` once a conversation has at least one replayed assistant turn that carried reasoning. The issue's local hotfix walks `messages[]` and `input[]` and strips the same 4 field names.

- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 22.x. Probe at `/tmp/probe_83812.mjs` (a) parses the patched `stream.ts` and verifies the field list is complete, the per-message walker is declared, and `stripReasoningParams` iterates both `messages[]` and `input[]` while preserving the original root-level deletions; (b) replays the patched strip against a realistic payload — root-level `reasoning` / `reasoning_effort`, a replayed assistant message with all 4 reasoning shapes, an array-shaped user content part with `reasoning` leakage, and an `input[]` entry — and asserts every reasoning field is removed while non-reasoning fields (`role`, `content`, `text`, `type`) survive; (c) replays the pre-fix shape (root-only strip) and confirms it leaves `messages[0].reasoning_details` intact — the exact field path the provider rejects.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node /tmp/probe_83812.mjs`

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
PASS: all 4 per-message reasoning field names declared
PASS: stripPerMessageReasoning helper declared
PASS: stripReasoningParams iterates payload.messages
PASS: stripReasoningParams iterates payload.input
PASS: root-level strip preserved (no regression vs prior behavior)
PASS: replay: realistic payload strips all 4 reasoning shapes from messages[], messages[].content[], and input[], preserves non-reasoning fields
PASS: old shape (root-only strip) preserves messages[].reasoning_details — confirms issue's HTTP 400 path

ALL CASES PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** A multi-turn opencode-go/kimi-k2.6 chat that previously hit `400 Extra inputs are not permitted, field: 'messages[N].reasoning_details'` no longer carries any reasoning-shaped field at any depth of the request payload (root, `messages[]`, `messages[].content[]`, `input[]`). Models outside the Kimi no-reasoning set (e.g. `deepseek-v4-pro`) short-circuit before the wrapper runs, so the strip never touches their payloads.

- **What was not tested:** Live request against `https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1` with `kimi-k2.6` after a multi-turn replay — that requires an opencode-go account and is what the reporter's local hotfix confirmed. The probe replays both the patched and pre-fix shapes against the exact field path named in the provider error, and the vitest regression cases exercise the public `createOpencodeGoKimiNoReasoningWrapper` contract on the same shape.

## Audit (per CLAUDE rules — all 5 steps)

- **Existing-helper check:** The reasoning-field list (`reasoning_details`, `reasoning_content`, `reasoning`, `reasoning_text`) is the same set as `COMPLETIONS_REASONING_REPLAY_FIELDS` in `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:2857`, but that constant is module-private. Inlining a 4-name const in this plugin file matches the established pattern of self-contained provider wrappers in `extensions/`. The strip is implemented via the existing `streamWithPayloadPatch` helper already imported here — no new framework. PASS
- **Shared-helper caller check:** `stripReasoningParams` has exactly one caller (the wrapper that closes over it, two lines below). Behavior change is additive: the new per-message walk runs only on payloads that already pass the `isOpencodeGoKimiNoReasoningModelId` gate. PASS
- **Broader-fix rival scan:** `gh pr list --search '83812 in:title,body'` and `gh pr list --search 'reasoning_details in:title,body'` both return no open PRs. PASS
- **Recent-merge audit:** `git log --oneline -10 -- extensions/opencode-go/stream.ts` shows `e1061a8b46 test(live): tolerate provider drift in release checks` — unrelated. PASS
- **Prototype-pollution scan:** The walker reads `message.content` and the message keys, then uses `delete msg[FIELD]` where `FIELD` comes from a hard-coded constant. No external-input key copying. PASS

target.title: fix(ollama): default unknown capabilities to tools
target.body:
## Summary

Ollama models can lose tools when OpenClaw cannot read their capability list.
This change makes that fallback assume native Ollama chat supports tools.
It keeps the fix limited to the one issue reproduced from #81915.

## What Changed

The fallback model definition now marks unknown Ollama capabilities as tool-capable.
Models that explicitly report capabilities still use the reported tools flag.

- Set supportsTools to true when capabilities are unavailable.
- Added a regression assertion for that fallback path.

## Testing

I tested the exact fallback path and the focused Ollama provider model test file.
I also checked formatting and whitespace.

- node --import tsx --eval 'import { buildOllamaModelDefinition } from "./extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.ts"; const m = buildOllamaModelDefinition("qwen2.5:3b"); console.log(JSON.stringify(m.compat)); if (m.compat?.supportsTools !== true) process.exit(1);'
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.ts extensions/ollama/src/provider-models.test.ts
- git diff --check

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw omitted supportsTools when Ollama capabilities were unavailable, so an Ollama model could be treated as not tool-capable.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.1.0, with a local Ollama-compatible HTTP endpoint that returned /api/tags and /api/show without a capabilities field.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran pnpm openclaw models list --provider ollama --json with OPENCLAW_HOME and OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH pointed at the local endpoint, then built the Ollama provider against the same endpoint.
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the OpenClaw CLI and provider runtime:

```text
OpenClaw CLI command:
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs models list --provider ollama --json
{
  "count": 1,
  "models": [
    {
      "key": "ollama/fallback-tools:latest",
      "name": "fallback-tools:latest",
      "input": "text",
      "contextWindow": 8192,
      "local": true,
      "available": false,
      "tags": [],
      "missing": false
    }
  ]
}

Ollama provider runtime output:
{
  "model": {
    "id": "fallback-tools:latest",
    "input": [
      "text"
    ],
    "contextWindow": 8192,
    "compat": {
      "supportsTools": true,
      "supportsUsageInStreaming": true
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: The CLI discovered the Ollama model from the endpoint whose /api/show response had no capabilities field, and the provider model still reported compat.supportsTools as true.
What was not tested: No additional behavior beyond the Ollama unknown-capabilities fallback in this PR.

## Risks

The main risk is an Ollama model that truly rejects tools when capabilities cannot be read.
That case can still opt out with compat.supportsTools: false on the model entry.

commands.author: kays0x

target.title: fix(agents): count user turns instead of stat for embedded session takeover fence
target.body:
## Summary

The embedded attempt session-lock fence uses a `stat()`-based fingerprint (`dev`, `ino`, `size`, `mtimeNs`, `ctimeNs`) to detect "another lane queued a new user turn against this session" while the runner releases its coarse lock for the LLM prompt window. The fingerprint is a proxy that overfires: every transcript-append the runner itself performs during the released window — `appendSessionTranscriptMessageLocked` writes tool calls, tool results, and assistant replies via `acquireSessionWriteLock(..., allowReentrant: true)` — changes the file's `size`/`mtimeNs`/`ctimeNs` without going through `refreshSessionFileFence`. Long DM turns with tool calls then trip `assertSessionFileFence` and throw `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` after the reply has already been delivered, surfacing a misleading "Agent failed before reply" message on top of a successful turn.

Keep the stat fingerprint as the fast path. On mismatch, only tolerate the runner's append-only assistant/tool transcript shape; treat everything else as a real takeover. The verification requires ALL of:

- **dev/ino match** — no atomic replacement onto a new inode.
- **birthtimeNs matches** — catches the unlink+recreate-same-inode case (common on tmpfs/ext4 with rapid recreation, where dev and ino are reused but the inode itself is fresh).
- **size grew** — file is append-only between fence-set and assert.
- **bytes `[0, fenceSize)` hash identical to the fenced prefix hash** — no in-place rewrite of earlier transcript content (catches compaction-style rewrites that happen to land on a same-byte boundary).
- **bytes `[fenceSize, currentSize)` are message entries with role `assistant` or `tool`** — no new user-role turn, no malformed tail.

Anything else — replacement, shrink, in-place rewrite, new user turn, or compaction — remains a real takeover. The fence snapshot captures the prefix SHA-256 alongside the fingerprint at `releaseForPrompt` and `refreshSessionFileFence` so the slow path can verify rigorously without keeping a file handle open.

Closes #83436.
Refs #83615 (this PR addresses the EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError surface; the issue also tracks unrelated kimi-k2.6 schema and DNS-fetch failures).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Embedded runner false-positive `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` fires after long Telegram DM turns whose reply was already delivered, surfacing a misleading "Agent failed before reply" message on top of a successful run.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw 2026.5.19-beta.1, Linux x86_64, Node 24.13.0. Long-running Telegram DM session against `claude-max-20x/claude-opus-4-7` (~3.5 min turn with multiple `exec` and `message` tool calls), reproduced reliably before this patch. Production behavior probed via runtime-patched `dist/selection-BpjGe-Y0.js` carrying the same fix semantics, then long DM runs (41s–122s) exercised through the Telegram channel post-restart.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
CI=true pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts
CI=true pnpm check:changed --staged=false
openclaw gateway restart
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Pre-patch failure mode, redacted runtime log from gateway (`/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-19.log`):
```
03:30:06.971  embedded run agent end: runId=bbd7d487 isError=false
03:30:07.008  embedded run prompt end: runId=bbd7d487 sessionId=<session-id> durationMs=217236
03:30:07.054  lane task error: lane=main durationMs=219867 error="EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: /home/.../agents/main/sessions/<session-id>.jsonl"
03:30:07.071  Embedded agent failed before reply: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: /home/.../<session-id>.jsonl
```
Note `isError=false` at `03:30:06.971` — the assistant turn and `message` tool delivery both succeeded — followed 47ms later by the takeover throw, surfacing as a misleading Telegram error on top of a delivered reply.

Post-patch behavior on the same gateway, four long DM runs after gateway restart at 03:46 EDT, every one delivering cleanly with zero takeover errors (redacted runtime log):
```
03:51:18  embedded run prompt end: runId=62395ffd  durationMs=122344  (no takeover)
03:52:39  embedded run prompt end: runId=67e21652  durationMs=41345   (no takeover)
04:05:05  embedded run prompt end: runId=a5994980  durationMs=101810  (no takeover)
04:07:34  embedded run prompt end: runId=a837440b  durationMs=94395   (no takeover)
```
The post-patch window contains only two "Embedded agent failed before reply" entries (at 04:05:06 and 04:07:35), both with cause `LLM request timed out` — unrelated to the takeover path. Zero `SessionTakeoverError` occurrences post-patch.

**Observed result after fix**: The renamed existing test (`rejects post-prompt writes when another owner queues a new user turn`) still asserts the takeover when a `role: "user"` entry is appended during the released window. Three new tests cover the runner's own assistant/tool appends (pass cleanly), in-place rewrite (rejected via prefix hash mismatch), and unlink+recreate-same-inode replacement (rejected via `birthtimeNs` mismatch even when the prefix bytes coincidentally match and the tail looks runner-owned). Targeted Vitest output:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
   Start at  04:24:31
   Duration  884ms
```
`check:changed --staged=false` is clean: 0 lint warnings/errors across 8647 files, 0 import cycles, all typecheck/build/security/quality lanes pass.

**What was not tested**: Multi-process write contention on the same `sessionFile` from a different gateway process (no test infrastructure for cross-process locks in this surface). The prefix-hash check is `O(fenceSize)` I/O per fence-set; in production sessions seen so far (≤ ~150 KB) this is sub-millisecond.

## Notes

- Adds three helpers near `readSessionFileFingerprint`: `FenceSnapshot` type, `snapshotSessionFileFence` (used at `releaseForPrompt` and `refreshSessionFileFence`), and `verifyAppendOnlyRunnerOwnedExtension` (used in the slow path of `assertSessionFileFence`). No new module-level dependencies beyond `node:crypto`'s `createHash`.
- Replaces one piece of controller state (`fenceFingerprint: SessionFileFingerprint | undefined`) with `fenceSnapshot: FenceSnapshot | undefined`. No new long-lived resources (no held file handles).
- This is the second revision of #84046; the first revision (count-only check) was flagged P1 by ClawSweeper for accepting same-user-count rewrites/replacements. This revision addresses that finding directly.

target.title: Add native subagent completion ownership
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #80450.

This PR adds an explicit native subagent completion ownership contract so thread-bound long-running subagent work can distinguish:

- `work-thread-final`: the bound work thread owns the complete final summary.
- `requester-session-final`: the requester session owns the complete final summary.
- `origin-bridge-final`: completion is intentionally bridged back through the requester/origin route.
- `none`: no automatic complete final summary is requested.

The change keeps legacy behavior for records without `completionOwner`: missing/legacy owner values still use the existing child-binding-first completion route.

## What changed

- Add a typed native subagent `completionOwner` contract.
- Persist `completionOwner` through subagent run records and pending final-delivery retry payloads.
- Derive native thread-bound spawn delivery behavior from `completionOwner`.
- Update completion routing so explicit requester/origin-owned finals skip the child/work-thread binding and route through the requester side instead.
- Reject `completionOwner` for `runtime="acp"` until ACP completion ownership semantics are implemented.
- Add regression coverage for requester/origin final ownership, native forwarding, ACP rejection, persistence, and owner resolution.

## Validation

Validation was limited to an isolated source checkout. No live gateway, config, or runtime-state tests were run.

Passed:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/subagent-completion-owner.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.thread-binding.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`
  - 9 files / 180 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`
  - 2 files / 68 tests passed after the final schema/test hardening.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed files>`
  - 0 warnings / 0 errors.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --type-check <changed runtime files>`
  - 0 warnings / 0 errors.
- `corepack pnpm check:test-types`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Native subagent final-summary ownership now has an explicit contract: `work-thread-final` keeps the complete final in the bound work thread; `requester-session-final` and `origin-bridge-final` route the complete final through the requester/origin side; `none` requests no automatic complete final; missing legacy owner values keep the previous child-binding-first route.
- **Real environment tested:** Isolated non-production OpenClaw source checkout on Linux. The proof used synthetic thread bindings and synthetic delivery targets against the real completion-owner resolver, native subagent delivery selection, and retry/delivery data-shape seams. No production Gateway, live runtime state, live config, or real channel was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run   src/agents/subagent-completion-owner.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-spawn.thread-binding.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts   src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run <generated non-production completionOwner proof test>
git diff --check
node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the isolated proof harness showed the ownership matrix and synthetic requester delivery path:

```text
[nonprod completionOwner route matrix] [
  {"case":"legacy-missing-owner","completionOwner":"<missing>","resolvedTo":"channel:synthetic-child-thread"},
  {"case":"work-thread-final","completionOwner":"work-thread-final","resolvedTo":"channel:synthetic-child-thread"},
  {"case":"requester-session-final","completionOwner":"requester-session-final","resolvedTo":"channel:synthetic-requester-thread"},
  {"case":"origin-bridge-final","completionOwner":"origin-bridge-final","resolvedTo":"channel:synthetic-requester-thread"}
]
[nonprod completionOwner delivery proof] {
  "ownerRows":[
    {"label":"thread-bound default","owner":"work-thread-final"},
    {"label":"requester final default","owner":"requester-session-final"},
    {"label":"explicit none","owner":"none"}
  ],
  "deliveryPath":"direct",
  "delivered":true,
  "gatewayMethod":"agent",
  "gatewayDeliver":true,
  "gatewayTo":"channel:synthetic-requester-thread"
}
Test Files 2 passed (2)
Tests 4 passed (4)

Targeted native subagent ownership suite: 9 files / 222 tests passed.
git diff --check: passed.
conflict-marker scan: passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The synthetic route matrix keeps missing/legacy and `work-thread-final` completions on the work-thread side, while `requester-session-final` and `origin-bridge-final` resolve to the requester side. The delivery proof then verifies requester-owned final delivery uses the requester route and records a direct synthetic delivery to the requester thread. The targeted native subagent suite also covers owner persistence, thread-bound spawn behavior, announce delivery, and spawn-tool validation.
- **What was not tested:** This is non-production proof. It does not exercise the production Gateway, production runtime state, production config, or any real Discord/Telegram/other channel. ACP completion ownership remains out of scope; this PR rejects `completionOwner` for ACP runtime rather than claiming ACP support.

## Scope boundary

This PR intentionally implements native subagent ownership only. ACP support is deferred; callers that pass `completionOwner` with `runtime="acp"` now receive an explicit error instead of silently getting misleading behavior.

target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #77969
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TTS can turn emoji and decorative symbols into noisy spoken names. Users who prefer cleaner voice output need a config-level way to strip those characters before synthesis without changing default behavior for everyone else.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `tts-skip-v3`, commit `06545b841d08`, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (`extensions/microsoft` -> `node-edge-tts` -> `speech.platform.bing.com`). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- After-fix evidence:
  ```text
  $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
  # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
  TS
  {
    "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
    "commit": "06545b841d08",
    "provider": "microsoft",
    "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
    "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
    "observed": [
      {
        "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
        "bytes": 39456,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      },
      {
        "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true.mp3",
        "bytes": 36288,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      }
    ],
    "emptyAfterStrip": {
      "success": false,
      "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
      "provider": null
    },
    "artifactsDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof"
  }

  $ ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration,size ...
  ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       32 passed (32)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       14 passed (14)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing `✓`, `©`, and `🫠`; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The original implementation filtered only some TTS entry points and did not guard the post-filter text before synthesis. The telephony path computed filtered text for setup but still passed the original text into provider synthesis, and emoji-only input could become an empty provider request.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - file synthesis strips complete emoji/symbol sequences when enabled.
  - stream synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - telephony synthesis receives stripped text when enabled.
  - telephony synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - tool-level config still passes original text into the shared TTS path, where effective config resolution owns filtering.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can enable `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` to strip emoji/symbol characters before speech synthesis. Unset or `false` preserves existing behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
text -> effective TTS config -> optional emoji/symbol strip -> empty guard -> provider synthesis
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: live proof speech-provider plugin plus focused Vitest coverage
- Integration/channel: speech-core file, stream, telephony, and TTS tool paths
- Relevant config: `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols=true|false`

### Steps

1. Run the live proof script shown above.
2. Run focused speech-core tests.
3. Run focused TTS tool tests.
4. Run test and production type gates.

### Expected

Enabled stripping removes emoji/symbols before provider synthesis and blocks empty-after-strip requests. Disabled mode preserves original text.

### Actual

The proof and tests match the expected behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Live speech-provider output for enabled, disabled, stream, telephony, and empty-after-strip paths.
- Focused speech-core test output.
- Focused TTS tool test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: enabled/disabled filtering, file synthesis, stream synthesis, telephony synthesis, empty-after-strip guard, adjacent TTS tool behavior.
- Edge cases checked: complete emoji/symbol sequences, emoji-only input, telephony provider request text, stream provider no-call behavior.
- Not verified: live vendor-specific pronunciation across every external provider.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: optional new config `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols`; no change required when unset.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Unicode stripping removes characters a user wanted spoken.
- Mitigation: behavior is opt-in and disabled by default.
- Risk: filtering differs across TTS paths.
- Mitigation: shared preparation is used before file, stream, and telephony synthesis, with regression tests for each path.

target.title: fix(agents): accept multi-line ANNOUNCE_SKIP on final line
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix `isAnnounceSkip()` to also accept multi-line sub-agent output where `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` appears as the final line after a newline, not just as the sole content.
- Add focused unit tests for `sessions-send-tokens.ts` covering exact, whitespace-padded, multi-line, and negative cases.
- Add an e2e regression test for the multi-line announce-skip scenario.

Closes #74071

## Problem

Sub-agents that produce a DM summary block followed by `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` on the final line had their entire summary leak into the parent session as an announcement. The strict equality check `(text ?? "").trim() === ANNOUNCE_SKIP_TOKEN` only matched when the model emitted *just* the token, but real sub-agent outputs typically end with the token after a summary block.

## Fix

`isAnnounceSkip()` now checks two conditions:
1. **Exact match** (existing): trimmed text equals the token
2. **Final-line match** (new): trimmed text ends with the token on its own line (after the last `\n`), with the final line also trimmed to handle whitespace

This preserves all existing behavior (exact and whitespace-padded) and adds the realistic multi-line case reported in the issue. The reporter had been running this patch locally since 2026-04-14 with no regressions.

## Test plan

- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tokens.test.ts` — 16 new focused unit tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts` — 9 existing tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts` — 3 existing tests (all pass)
- `pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` — 15 existing tests (all pass)

## Notes

- `isReplySkip()` has the same strict-equality pattern but is not changed here since there is no reported issue for it in the A2A ping-pong flow. It could receive the same treatment in a follow-up if needed.
- Docs currently describe exact-token suppression (`docs/tools/subagents.md`); consider aligning docs if final-line token support becomes the intended contract.

target.title: docs(providers): merge GLM docs into Z.AI page
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem:** Z.AI and GLM are actually the same provider in OpenClaw (`zai` provider, `ZAI_API_KEY`, Z.AI Chat Completions / Bearer auth), but the docs were split across `/providers/zai` (provider page) and `/providers/glm` (model-family page). The two pages duplicated setup guidance, drifted apart over time, and users entering from either side had to bounce between them to get the full picture. - **Solution:** Merge the GLM page into `docs/providers/zai.md`, delete `docs/providers/glm.md`, and point every internal link / nav entry / i18n nav / redirect at `/providers/zai`. While merging, add a Property table and a Config example to the Z.AI page, and simplify Getting started from 3 steps to 2 (onboarding already sets the default model, so the "Set a default model" step is no longer needed). - **What changed:** - `docs/providers/zai.md`: add Property table; add Config example section; correct the auto-detect wording (the `zai-api-key` choice **probes** supported endpoints with the API key — it does not infer from a key prefix); simplify Getting started from 3 to 2 steps; align Related cards with Model selection / Configuration reference. - Delete `docs/providers/glm.md` (content merged into `zai.md`). - `docs/docs.json`: change `/glm` destination from `/providers/glm` to `/providers/zai`; add new `/providers/glm` → `/providers/zai` redirect; remove `providers/glm` from navigation. - `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`: keep main's `` structure; rename the GLM card to `Z.AI (GLM)` and update href to `/providers/zai`. - `docs/providers/index.md`, `docs/providers/models.md`, `docs/concepts/oauth.md`, `docs/help/faq-first-run.md`, `docs/providers/anthropic.md`, `docs/.i18n/zh-Hans-navigation.json`: every reference to `glm` / `providers/glm` updated to `providers/zai`. - **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Pure docs. No changes to runtime / `extensions/zai` / `src/plugins/provider-zai-endpoint.ts` / any plugin / CI / config schema. No standalone stub page kept at `/providers/glm` — the redirect in `docs.json` handles it. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra (Docs-only PR. UI / DX is checked because this is the first-touch onboarding doc for the Z.AI/GLM provider and directly shapes the new-user setup flow.) ## Linked Issue/PR No prior issue — submitted as a direct contribution. - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Motivation The motivation comes from a real onboarding gap: many entry points (`docs/concepts/model-providers.md`, `docs/providers/index.md`, `docs/concepts/oauth.md`, `docs/help/faq-first-run.md`) surface `GLM models` first and route users to `/providers/glm`, which had its own self-contained setup. But the actual OpenClaw provider is `zai` (`ZAI_API_KEY`, Z.AI Chat Completions), so half of the operational information also lived under `/providers/zai`. Over time the two pages drifted, and "GLM is a model family hosted by Z.AI" — a key conceptual point — was hard to express cleanly across two separate pages. Consolidating to a single Z.AI page makes that relationship explicit at the top of the page and removes a recurring source of new-user confusion. The merge also lets us reflect the current onboarding behavior accurately: `openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-api-key` (and the regional `zai-coding-*` / `zai-cn` variants) already sets the default model, so the old "Set a default model" doc step is now stale and is removed in this PR. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Doc split between Z.AI and GLM produced two competing entry points and stale links. After the merge, every legacy URL (`/glm`, `/providers/glm`, `/zai`) must redirect to `/providers/zai`; the Z.AI page must render the full GLM model catalog and the merged Getting started; nav / providers index must not show a standalone GLM entry. - **Real environment tested:** Local Mintlify dev preview. `npx --yes mintlify dev` on Linux, Node v24.15.0, started from `docs/` in a clean checkout of this branch. `docs/docs.json` uses Mintlify schema `https://mintlify.com/docs.json`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash cd docs npx --yes mintlify dev # In another terminal, once the preview is ready on localhost:3000: curl -sI http://localhost:3000/glm | grep -iE 'HTTP|location' curl -sI http://localhost:3000/providers/glm | grep -iE 'HTTP|location' curl -sI http://localhost:3000/zai | grep -iE 'HTTP|location' curl -sI http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -iE 'HTTP' curl -s http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -oiE ']*>[^<]*' curl -s http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -oiE 'glm-[0-9.a-z-]+|z\.ai|coding[- ]plan' | sort -u ``` - **Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):** Terminal output from the local Mintlify preview (verbatim): ``` $ curl -sI http://localhost:3000/glm | grep -iE 'HTTP|location' HTTP/1.1 307 Temporary Redirect location: /providers/zai $ curl -sI http://localhost:3000/providers/glm | grep -iE 'HTTP|location' HTTP/1.1 307 Temporary Redirect location: /providers/zai $ curl -sI http://localhost:3000/zai | grep -iE 'HTTP|location' HTTP/1.1 307 Temporary Redirect location: /providers/zai $ curl -sI http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -iE 'HTTP' HTTP/1.1 200 OK $ curl -s http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -oiE ']*>[^<]*' Z.AI - OpenClaw $ curl -s http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -oiE 'glm-[0-9.a-z-]+|z\.ai|coding[- ]plan' | sort -u Coding Plan Z.AI glm-4.5-air glm-4.5-flash glm-4.7-flash glm-4.7-flashx glm-5-models glm-5-turbo glm-models z.ai ``` Optional screenshot of the rendered Z.AI page (terminal evidence above is sufficient; this is supplementary): - **Observed result after fix:** - `/glm`, `/providers/glm`, and `/zai` all return `307 Temporary Redirect` with `location: /providers/zai`. (Production Mintlify may use a different redirect status, but the destination is the same and behavior is equivalent.) - `/providers/zai` returns `200 OK` with `Z.AI - OpenClaw`. - The rendered HTML contains the full GLM model list (`glm-4.5-air`, `glm-4.5-flash`, `glm-4.7-flash`, `glm-4.7-flashx`, `glm-5-turbo`, `glm-5-models`, `glm-models`) and `Z.AI` / `Coding Plan` sections — the original `glm.md` content has been merged in without loss. - Mintlify dev starts with no broken-link warnings; all previous links to `providers/glm` have been updated to `providers/zai`. - **What was not tested:** Production Mintlify CDN cache. If any stale `/providers/glm` cache objects exist on the production deployment, they will either expire naturally or need to be purged on the deployment side — out of scope for this PR. - **Before evidence (optional but encouraged):** The "before" state can be observed on the current production deployment: https://docs.openclaw.ai/providers/glm and https://docs.openclaw.ai/providers/zai are still two separate pages with duplicated setup that has drifted apart. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Visitors to `/providers/glm` or `/glm` are redirected to `/providers/zai`. (`/glm` previously redirected to `/providers/glm`; the destination is updated. `/providers/glm` is a new redirect.) - The docs site navigation and providers index no longer show a standalone `GLM models` entry. `Z.AI` is the single entry point and its subtitle mentions GLM. - The Z.AI page now has a Property table and a Config example. Getting started is simplified from 3 steps (onboard → set default model → verify) to 2 steps (onboard → verify), reflecting that onboarding already sets the default model. - The endpoint auto-detect description is corrected: the `zai-api-key` choice probes supported endpoints with the API key, rather than inferring from a key prefix. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **No** - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** Docs-only — no runtime / auth / network behavior changes. ## Repro + Verification See §Real behavior proof above — Environment, Steps, Expected, and Actual are all covered there. - OS: Linux (Ubuntu) - Runtime/container: Node v24.15.0; `npx --yes mintlify dev` (no global install) - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): `docs/docs.json` (Mintlify schema) ## Evidence - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets — see Real behavior proof / Evidence after fix - [ ] Screenshot/recording — optional; terminal evidence is sufficient - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: - All three legacy URLs (`/glm`, `/providers/glm`, `/zai`) correctly redirect to `/providers/zai` in the local Mintlify preview. - The rendered `/providers/zai` page contains the full GLM model list and the merged Getting started content. - Mintlify dev starts with no broken-link warnings. - Branch hygiene: ahead 1 commit relative to `origin/main`, diff limited to 10 files under `docs/`, no runtime / extensions / UI / superpowers files leaked into the PR. - Edge cases checked: - Both `/glm` (existing redirect, destination updated) and `/providers/glm` (newly added redirect) paths are exercised. - The Chinese navigation `docs/.i18n/zh-Hans-navigation.json` also has `zh-CN/providers/glm` removed. - What was **not** verified: - CDN cache purge on the production docs.openclaw.ai deployment (deployment-side concern). - Whether every GLM model id string in the page still corresponds to a callable model on Z.AI today — this PR only consolidates docs and does not change the bundled `zai` manifest. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? **Yes** — legacy URLs still resolve via redirects. - Config/env changes? **No** - Migration needed? **No** ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: If the production Mintlify deployment has CDN-cached `/providers/glm` responses, users may see stale content briefly. - Mitigation: Cached entries will expire naturally, or a maintainer can purge them on the deployment side. The redirect itself is in `docs.json` and takes effect immediately once cache is cleared. - Risk: External blog posts or old GitHub issues may still link to `/providers/glm`. - Mitigation: The `docs.json` redirect covers both `/providers/glm` and `/glm`, so external links continue to land users on the consolidated page. No external-link fix-up needed.

target.title: fix(slack): keep agent display name and avatar on edited messages
target.body:
### Linked Issue

- Closes #58737
- Issue URL: `https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/58737`

### Summary

- Fix Slack edit flows so agent `username` and avatar identity are preserved when using `chat.update`.
- Apply identity forwarding across draft stream, preview finalization, and status-final completion update paths.
- Add regression tests to lock behavior for send/edit identity propagation.

### What Changed

1. `extensions/slack/src/actions.ts`
   - Extended `editSlackMessage` to accept `identity` and forward `username` plus avatar fields to `chat.update`.
   - Kept avatar payload mutually exclusive for `icon_url` vs `icon_emoji`.

2. `extensions/slack/src/draft-stream.ts`
   - Added optional `identity` to draft stream params.
   - Forwarded identity to both `sendMessageSlack` and `editSlackMessage` paths.

3. `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/preview-finalize.ts`
   - Added optional `identity` to finalization params.
   - Forwarded identity to `editSlackMessage` when converting preview into final message.

4. `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts`
   - Passed resolved `slackIdentity` into `finalizeSlackPreviewEdit`.
   - Updated `status_final` completion edit to use `editSlackMessage` with identity instead of raw `chat.update`.
   - Passed `identity` into `createSlackDraftStream`.

5. Tests
   - `extensions/slack/src/actions.blocks.test.ts`: added edit identity preservation assertion.
   - `extensions/slack/src/draft-stream.test.ts`: added identity forwarding test for send/edit.
   - `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/preview-finalize.test.ts`: added identity passthrough assertion.

### User-visible Behavior Change

- Slack messages edited by OpenClaw now keep the configured agent display name and avatar instead of reverting to the bot default identity.

### Test Plan

- Executed targeted tests:
  - `pnpm test -- extensions/slack/src/actions.blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/draft-stream.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/preview-finalize.test.ts`
- Result:
  - `3` test files passed, `22` tests passed.
- Formatting:
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` passed for all touched Slack files.

### Risks / Notes

- Identity forwarding depends on Slack app token scopes (for example `chat:write.customize`) when custom identity fields are used.
- No config schema changes; this is a behavior fix for existing edit paths.

target.title: fix(memory-host): add AbortSignal support to batch polling (OpenAI, Voyage, Gemini)
target.body:
Add AbortSignal support to batch polling loops to enable immediate cancellation when clients disconnect.

Problem
When HTTP clients disconnect during batch embedding operations, server-side polling continues until timeout, wasting API quota.

Solution
- Replace uncancelable setTimeout with cancellable version using clearTimeout
- Add signal?: AbortSignal parameter to all batch providers
- Use { once: true } to prevent listener accumulation (fixes MaxListenersExceededWarning)
- Thread signal through full call chain: batch-runner → runXxxBatches → waitForXxxBatch

Changes
- batch-runner.ts: Add signal to EmbeddingBatchExecutionParams type
- batch-openai.ts: Add signal support with {once: true} cleanup
- batch-voyage.ts: Update sleep type, add signal threading through deps
- batch-gemini.ts: Add signal support with {once: true} cleanup

Testing
- Verified polling stops within 1s of abort (was 30+ iterations)
- No listener leaks after long polls
- TypeScript compiles without errors

Backwards Compatible: signal is optional.

**Additional Fixes (Post-Review)**:
- Fixed `initial: batchInfo` parameter regression in `batch-voyage.ts` and `batch-gemini.ts` to avoid extra API calls on batch creation
- Fixed locale regression in `ui/src/ui/presenter.ts` (reverted hardcoded "en-US" to `undefined` for i18n compliance)

target.title: chore: add .history/ to .gitignore
target.body:
## Description

Add `.history/` to `.gitignore` to exclude editor-generated history files.

## Motivation

Trae and other code editors automatically create a `.history/` directory 
for local file versioning. This directory should not be committed to the 
repository.

## Changes

- Added `.history/` to `.gitignore`

## Checklist

- [x] No breaking changes
- [x] Follows project conventions

commands.author: stevenepalmer

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- The maintenance timer now reads current runtime config on each sweep, so hot config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are applied without a gateway restart.
- Docs now describe proactive daily rollover and the implicit provider-owned CLI session exemption.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #82321
- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A daily reset with `atHour` is a wall-clock policy. Users should not need to send a new message after the reset boundary before stale session context is cleared. The maintenance sweep should also honor the current runtime config after hot reloads, not the startup snapshot.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Daily reset freshness was only evaluated through session-use paths. If no message arrived after `atHour`, the stale session could remain active even though the configured reset time had passed. The initial scheduler also captured the startup config, which meant hot reloads could leave the sweep using stale reset policy until restart.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - A stale daily session resets during a scheduler sweep without a new message.
  - A fresh daily session is left alone.
  - Implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are not cut by the default daily policy.
  - Sessions with active runs are skipped.
  - Scheduler sweeps use current runtime config after a reset-policy change.
- Existing coverage before this patch covered stale detection when called, but not proactive scheduled execution or reload-aware maintenance behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Configured daily session resets now happen on schedule during gateway uptime, even if no message arrives after the reset boundary. The public docs now state that proactive rollover can happen from gateway maintenance and that implicit provider-owned CLI sessions keep continuity unless reset is explicit.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
daily reset boundary
  -> gateway maintenance sweep
  -> read current runtime config
  -> skip active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions
  -> stale daily sessions reset through existing reset service
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux container
- Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: `session.reset.mode=daily`, `session.reset.atHour=4`

### Steps

1. Create a session store entry with `sessionStartedAt` before the daily reset boundary.
2. Run the daily reset scheduler scan with `nowMs` after the reset boundary.
3. Confirm the reset callback runs for the stale session.
4. Repeat with a fresh post-boundary session and confirm it is not reset.
5. Mark a stale session key active and confirm it is skipped.
6. Add provider-owned CLI continuity under the implicit reset policy and confirm it is skipped.
7. Change the scheduler's runtime config from a noon reset boundary to a 4 AM reset boundary and confirm the next sweep uses the new policy.

### Expected

Stale daily sessions reset without inbound traffic; fresh, active, and implicitly provider-owned CLI sessions remain unchanged; config reloads affect future scheduler sweeps.

### Actual

The focused scheduler and gateway maintenance tests match that behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Focused scheduler and maintenance test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.
- Docs updated in:
  - `docs/concepts/session.md`
  - `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`
  - `docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md`

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: stale daily reset scheduler path, fresh-session no-op path, active-run skip, implicit provider-owned CLI skip, runtime-config reload path, gateway maintenance timer lifecycle.
- Edge cases checked: scheduler skips overlapping sweeps; shutdown/runtime cleanup clears the timer; type gates pass.
- Not verified: a live gateway instance naturally crossing the configured reset hour.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the gateway now scans configured session stores every minute.
- Mitigation: scans are bounded to discovered session stores, skip overlapping runs, and only call reset for stale daily sessions.
- Risk: deployments that assumed lazy daily resets may observe earlier reset behavior.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock expectation for `mode: daily` and `atHour`.
- Risk: hot config changes could otherwise make the scheduler use stale reset policy.
- Mitigation: future sweeps call the gateway runtime config getter before scanning.

target.title: fix: drop replayed tool-call stubs from malformed assistant history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: provider-switched or malformed assistant transcript entries can replay literal `[TOOL CALL STUB]` placeholder text back into context.
- Why it matters: those stub-only turns are dead transcript artifacts, not meaningful memory, and they can pollute replayed context after provider changes.
- What changed: `sanitizeSessionHistory` now strips tool-call stub placeholders from malformed assistant string content and drops assistant entries that are stub-only after cleanup.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change transcript storage format, provider translation, or tool-call/result pairing rules for valid structured history.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: malformed assistant history entries with string `content` were canonicalized into plain text replay messages without filtering known transcript placeholder artifacts.
- Missing detection / guardrail: replay sanitization covered malformed assistant content shape, but not the specific case where that malformed content was only a tool-call placeholder.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): current replay sanitizer already canonicalizes malformed assistant content in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/google.ts`, and existing tests cover that generic path plus provider-switch replay edge cases.
- Why this regressed now: provider switching can leave transcript artifacts that are valid strings structurally, so they bypassed stricter tool-call normalization and survived as replayable text.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: this fix does not rely on changing persisted session format or provider-specific tool ID logic; existing OpenAI tool ID preservation coverage still passes.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: malformed assistant string content containing `[TOOL CALL STUB]` is either dropped when stub-only or preserved as prose with the placeholder removed.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the behavior lives entirely inside replay sanitization and does not require provider IO or session file rewriting to reproduce.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.openai-tool-id-preservation.test.ts` was rerun to confirm adjacent replay behavior still holds.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users no longer get replay context polluted by literal `[TOOL CALL STUB]` assistant history placeholders after malformed/provider-switched transcript history is sanitized.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[malformed assistant string: "[TOOL CALL STUB]"] -> canonicalized to replayable text block

After:
[malformed assistant string: "[TOOL CALL STUB]"] -> recognized as placeholder-only -> dropped
[malformed assistant string: "Before [TOOL CALL STUB] after"] -> placeholder stripped -> "Before after"
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node/Vitest
- Model/provider: replay sanitizer coverage for `openai` plus adjacent OpenAI replay regression
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): default unit test harness

### Steps

1. Create malformed assistant replay history with string content equal to `[TOOL CALL STUB]`.
2. Run `sanitizeSessionHistory(...)` for replay.
3. Repeat with mixed prose plus placeholder text.

### Expected

- Stub-only assistant history is not replayed.
- Mixed prose remains replayable, but the placeholder is removed.

### Actual

- Before this change, malformed assistant string content was canonicalized directly into replay text, preserving the placeholder artifact.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: targeted replay sanitizer test suite and adjacent OpenAI tool ID preservation regression test.
- Edge cases checked: stub-only placeholder entry, prose surrounding placeholder, existing OpenAI replay ID preservation.
- What you did **not** verify: full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` repo-wide lane.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: placeholder stripping could remove literal user-authored text if an assistant malformed history string intentionally contains `[TOOL CALL STUB]`.
  - Mitigation: the change is limited to malformed assistant replay entries already on the canonicalization fallback path, and the cleanup only removes the specific placeholder token.

target.title: feat(cli): add --dry-run flag to cron add
target.body:
## Summary

- add `--dry-run` to `openclaw cron add`
- build and print the resolved params object without calling `cron.add`
- emit human-readable output by default, or the raw params object with `--json`
- add CLI tests covering preview output, JSON output, and the no-RPC path

## Why

Users currently have to create a cron job before they can verify that the resolved schedule and payload are what they intended. `--dry-run` gives them a safe way to validate the command before persisting anything.

## Scope

- CLI only
- no scheduler logic changes
- no gateway contract changes
- no workflow / release / docs automation changes

## Behavior

- `openclaw cron add ... --dry-run` prints the params that would be sent to the gateway and exits
- `openclaw cron add ... --dry-run --json` emits the plain params object
- validation still runs before output, so invalid flag combinations still fail normally

## Testing

- `src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-add.test.ts`
- verifies preview output on `--dry-run`
- verifies `--dry-run --json` writes a plain object
- verifies the gateway RPC is not called in either dry-run mode

## Related

Closes #59452

## Reviewer Note

This branch was rebased onto current `main` and cleaned up to remove unrelated workflow-sync changes from the earlier iteration of this PR.

commands.review_summary:
